A.does: Simulates the NFA using Thompson's algorithm, maintaining a set of all currently active NFA states and advancing them in lockstep through each input byte via epsilon-closure expansion.

B.does: Executes a depth-first recursive backtracking search over the NFA, but uses a visited bitset (capped at a configurable capacity, default 256 KB) to skip (state, position) pairs already explored, bounding worst-case behavior.

comparison: PikeVM is preferred for unbounded or large inputs where the visited-state budget of BoundedBacktracker would be exceeded; BoundedBacktracker is preferred for short inputs where its lower overhead and visited-bitset short-circuit make it faster.
notes: BoundedBacktracker in meta/wrappers.rs is thin wrapper; canonical at nfa/thompson/backtrack.rs:427
